hypothesis: Incorporating a dynamic knowledge base update mechanism—wherein the LLM's own verified reasoning explanations from successful compositional transitions are continuously appended to the knowledge base during the iterative design process—will outperform the static pre-constructed knowledge base used in MatAgent by enabling the system to accumulate task-specific composition-property relationships in real time, thereby achieving higher success rates and V.U.N. scores particularly for extreme target properties (e.g., formation energies at the 1% quantile) where the pre-built knowledge base derived from random MP-20 composition pairs provides insufficient coverage of the relevant chemical space.
